Comments: Super happy to have connor on my team (several times). Really knows what he’s doing, and I really like that he goes back to his designated spot most of the time as opposed to just whatever might feel easiest (where your momentum might take you). Takes feedback well and has improved even more.
He’s very good, but you already know that
Only had him one for one rotation, but for some reason he was presenting with both hands up even if he had only one ball or none?
Solid.
Flawless supertiebreak
rolls are silky smooth. Slightly too cautious on a few roll/wait decisions but prefer that to being risky. High effort, good crossing decisions. Good speed and athleticism
Really really good
Quick, sharp, efficient
Pretty solid for saturday!
Benjy is a human highlight reel. Just made a homerun robbery of a ball going over the fence on Harris.
sweet sweet snag on a missed first serve. Also he just threw a mahomes quick pitch to get the balance off in time. He’s in mid-tournament form in his 3rd shift of the day
He was spiderman out there today catching everything
lol i mean this guy is a menace. Tossed his rookie back in the deep end per usual. She handled it well
Doing ball last in play and correct decisionmaking
Tentative on rolling a ball down
A little hesitant but good awareness
Had another instance of not looking around her space to see if any balls had come her way. The camera on stadium. Chair umpire had to say something a few times to get her attention
looked really good out there for a second day rookie! She was showing for her back partner already, seemed comfortable with the pace of play.
No issues or anything during shift. Did fine!
for a first year on her first day, her first shift was amazing. still learning a couple things but she’s good at balancing, aware, good hands
Blocked sponsor on stadium
Needs to commit to getting balls. There were a couple times she held up play because she didn’t know where a ball went but then it ended up rolling onto court
Looks pretty good, has good tosses to player but wasn’t super confident in service change

Good balancing, kept track of score and good freeze frame. Did have one off court incident were she went with a friend to take a picture with a player (friend gave her a coat to cover her uniform) but she was not back in time for when her half of the shift went on to start the next match. Shapiro was looking for her on court and we were able to get her on court just before the players arrived but I took a star off for this situation

Doesn’t seem to be completely
Confident in what to do. Had to look at the other back to validate that it was a service change
Very good Saturday and today. Solid in everything
Overall good back. Could be a bit quicker to the ball.
Pretty good rookie for Saturday! Didn’t really notice anything she did poorly tbh.
Not bad, slightly above average for a rookie and hasn't made any big mistakes of note yet
already holding ball in play down consistently! She looks fantastic for a second day rookie, need to see more to feel comfortable with a 3 rating but she was good!
On top of everything
Totally fine out there
You're telling me this guy drives players AND gives them balls on the same day? What a talent

Has transitioned really well to Net
Good net but left the shift early
Good net but left the shift early
Overall pretty good and seems to have good awareness but makes small rookie mistakes and doesn’t anticipate
About as good as feasible
Not great for a second year. Slow, not always sure what to do.
Weird standing in the back but nothing tragic
rolled between serves. POACHES. Wasn’t paying attention in meeting and did not know when to come on court. Slow to get umbrellas up. Late to shift. Spaces out.
Late to multiple shifts. Roll in between serves. Needs to pay more attention while on court. Overly poaching and isn’t always sure where balls are supposed to go
runs fast but decision-making can be a bit slow, rolls could be better
totally space cadet-ed after warm up, Frankie had to get balls from both sides to the servers side and he never even noticed what was happening. Lacks court awareness.

Really solid and almost always doing the right thing. Only put three balls on service side one time on tiebreak
Great. Became accustomed to how our tournament do things and is now a proficient net.
completely on the ball. really quick
fumbled a pick up at net early. Making excellent decisions, compensating for a net partner that’s making some questionable decisions. Rolls are perfect. She looks excellent out there.
Did a nice job, missed some of the finer details but happy

Looked good at net. Showing hands, communicating with partner and backs. Good intensity. Sometimes she would overpoach/try to get balls the back would usually get. We talked about it while we were off. She's done other tournaments before so she's experienced, just still learning our rules.
Very solid!
DANG that's one good rookie!! Killed it in a doubles tiebreak and is consistently gaining confidence

good decision with net partner making some confusing choices. She’s also communicating really well, just using head nods and tilts but shes really communicating efficiently. Rolls are phenomenal and she’s fast. If she can reproduce this in another shift, she’s a 4.5 for me.
Olivia did a fantastic job today. She had great court awareness and was always asking for feedback. Asked a lot of good questions and her experience showed.
great net, fast, good rolls, early in the day poached but fixed after told

still learning our net rules, poaching a good number of balls and causing some confusion with her net partner (only reason I knock her down from a 3.5) Good awareness, great rolls, great speed/athleticism.
Good overall. Still adapting to the way our tournament does things.
Net - overall fairly smooth day. She was still getting used to the our net technique of crossing to the other side but had great rolls and good awareness
Fine but I consistently told him to balance or to look at me when he has 3+ balls and he continued to not. Didn’t balance, I felt like he didn’t implement much feedback
Fed ball last in play. Otherwise looks really good

good hands, athleticism, attitude. You can tell he’s still a rookie with some of the hesitations and minor mistakes in balancing. He’s looking pretty good tho, clearly paying attention to me and trying to do some of the things I do.
Nothing spectacular. Definitely rookie material. Nothing horrendous either.
Dang good rookie
Missed freeze frame. Granted, he’s a rookie
pretty strong rookie shift
Fantastic hands on court. Super quick and catching balls left and right. Looks super professional
One bad roll

I like this guy :) I would get mildly hospitalized for him
Very smooth across the board
Very good

Overall aggressive and thinks he knows more then the captains he is a good ball kid but has a tad of an attitude

Great performance as always. Was training his partner Scott.  Gave partner instruction what to do on even number ball change. They looked like pros out there.
Great shift on stadium. Only his second shift of the year but looked solid and great effort

I want to see higher intensity, it’s not bad but can be better. Really good hands. Later in the day he started cramping but really didn’t want to leave the court, like the effort there even tho maybe should’ve just rested.
Rating after first rotation. Low court awarness. Does not look for balancing throws. Unsure of score and when to roll balls to the other side. Little communication.
Did improvement slightly over the day, but still was having court awarness issues. Balancing and communicating did improve but there were still of significant hesitation
Chair ump had to pause play multiple times because of balls at his feet.
Hasn’t seemed to catch on to the players repetitive habits
Still having some awareness issues. Didn’t see the back balancing a ball and it went right by him
A few little miscues but not too bad

Was late to a shift. Constantly dozing off in the back. Multiple times where a ball was given back to him from the player and he didn’t see it. Had to have the other back come across the court to get the ball because he was looking at the sky.
Missed another ball at his feet which held up play. Second of the match

Main issue was that he was next to towels, wasn’t moving out at all to give net place to roll balls without hitting players feet. When net did roll there, he didn’t seem to realize this was a bad situation and try to stop balls

Main issue was that he was next to towels, wasn’t moving out at all to give net place to roll balls without hitting players feet. When net did roll there, he didn’t seem to realize this was a bad situation and try to stop balls
Decent first day. Slightly hesitant and still getting feel for how to react in different situations
Was about to roll balls to the other side after the first game in set but net stopped him
clearly a step behind in a really impressive team. Hesitant, unsure of himself pretty clearly. Looks like a nervous new rookie, should get better.
Back - probably our worst performer of the day. Seemed slow in his movements and did not really try to pay attention during our post shift briefings
everything you could ask for on and off court
slow. Rolls are bouncy and inaccurate 🤷🏻‍♂️
No mistakes
Did not come onto court with his back partner because the shift should have been over. A team did not come to replace yet he did not come onto court.
Deb can be lazy with his movement and court awareness.  He seemed distracted and during post match huddle was confrontational and did not take correction.  I didn’t expect that from a veteran.
Horrific shift in stadium. Face plant. Slow. Missed ball balances. Missed last ball down.
Pretty hard to put him out on court with just the huge athletic limitations.

Not sure if it was just because it was qualifying and he knew the cameras were off, but for some reason Deb was always standing in front of the advertisements? I even tried to communicate with him to move but it continued on all three court we were on...
very consistent as usual!
Solid performance. No complaints, very consistent on court
moving very slow today. Not sure if it was the heat or what but there wasn’t any sense of urgency in his movements. Knows what he’s doing.
Very good Back
Saw as net, where he did improve as shift went on.
Needs a lot of work at Net
Really good at back (think he’s better as a back than at net)
good hands! A little over-aggressive with going to net instead of back to his postion. Otherwise he was very good.
Not a good net. Better as a back. Spaces out at times

Overall was really good could’ve been a little less hesitant at times though

Decent day did have to caution him about throwing too hard to balance with his back partner. Thankfully Ethan was ready for one very powerful balance throw on Harris Court, otherwise the ball would have likely been in the stands
good back, aware, fast, good balancing
He looks really good out there. excellent throwing balances. Quick/decisive first move. I don’t really have anything negative to say, he’s getting really really good.
Really good rolls and balancing.
Absolutely lights out. Fast smooth, impeccable rolls
Great dude. Minor mistakes during first tiebreak, but was perfect on second tiebreak.
Does everything right
Could tell he’s been listening to our daily notes. Sprinted in and got the umbrella up immediately

Extremely solid, as one would expect from a multi-year vet of other tournaments. A treasure trove of info and a great mentor as well
Mildly bothers me with his habits from other tournaments lol but really good and helpful instructing
Kinda overwhelmed me with questions today, but a super nice guy! Solid on court and didn’t notice any glaring errors
Back - great mentor for our younger ballkids esp Max. Slightly limited physically but knows whats going on
Fine. Pretty average but I felt like she didn’t implement some feedbacks
She was fine but i felt like she didn’t improve a to  through the week, as opposed to a few other rookies
A passable back, although her weaknesses show quite strongly when with another rookie back. Lacks anticipation and is very hesitant to take initiative
isn’t making it obvious she has balls in her hand. Not putting last ball in play down

Great rookie!

Not the best. Very slow rolling that can be sped up. She also waits for you to pick up her roll before she rolls again. She def looks new on court. Could be a lot better but could also be a lot worse.
Better then yesterday but still not completely clicking
Solid balancing but could be slightly better. Could be slightly more aggressive but overall good

looks like a more nervous rookie. Inconsistent with balancing, just not quite upto the pace of play yet. I think she’ll be fine when she loses the nervous, she seems to be second guessing. Good effort tho and athleticism.

needs to step out from the wall. Was stuck at the wall and couldn’t roll balls down on even game. Doesn’t balance. Poor awareness. Takes too long to between rolls, slowing down even game ball changes. Doesn’t know when to roll.

Good back partner. Took a second to get the player patterns down, but once she did, she was great.
Not immediate recognition of balls coming her way, but otherwise flawless
Good hands and very aggressive. But was one half of a flub of not rolling the ball on an even game until the ump told them too
Pretty solid shift. Didn’t notice anything concerning. Seems to have a solid understanding
solid. Did have one issue where her back partner wanted the balls with the back in the center during serve warmup and ally wasn’t understanding what she wanted.
Not terrible. Hesitates or doesn’t go for balls between positions. Needs to improve rolls.

hesitating slightly at on balls down the middle and could take a few more steps off the back while servicing a player. Other than that she looks like she knows what she’s doing, good decisions and awareness.
Back - she showed no signs of rust and was able to get back into it. overall seemed like a very solid back
head moving during points. Needs to pay more attention to the score.  Also needs to bees hesitant when going for balls

Spaces out.

he looks so much more confident than he did last year. Always checking both ways. I feel like he could run a little bit faster but that’s a minor qualm. If I see one more shift like this from him hes a 3.5 star for me!

looks good, pleasantly surprised by him so far! Good decision making when he had a ball, ball went to receiving side and game ended. Was decisive in getting fan/umbrella up, clearly knew the score and player first.
Net - was getting back into the swing of things but had an good start. Misunderstood the score on a freeze frame
He ain't bad!
Doesn't look both ways, indecisive

He ain't bad! Made a lil goof on stadium but he aight
Frustrated with some of his decisions and lack of awareness. Felt like i was covering him a bit because I reacted more quickly than him at net
Limited athleticism, but actually seemed to get everything right on this shift.

looks solid still some hesitancy. tried to switch balls after first game of match, back caught it tho. Unnecessary run to back. Did ok with a very weak net partner. Looks like an ok second year. Love his enthusiasm/commitment tho!
Less effective shift. Missed last ball down. Missed player first. Balanced too late (player was bouncing ball)
Another great shift today to close out Harris court. Excellent balancing and court awarness.
Didn’t see him for long but looks great for a rookie. Very aggressive and looking to help out and improve.
Really strong rookie. Great job balancing and asking questions. Wants to learn and wants to do well. Good intensity.
Nice kid.  Pleasure to have on a team.
Effort sky high. Only thing is he is over eager at times. For example, so frantic to get back to spot that he crowds player toweling.
He’s surprisingly competent and capable for 1st year.
Excellent stadium shift. Did a great job handling the strange Azerenka quirk of wanting all 6 balls on the Ad side.
Excellent job today. Good awarness for a rookie and always looking to balance. Good rolls. Good court awarness and communication, always shoeing hands on the receiving side
Rating from 7/31 7pm stadium shift. Was very impressed. Handles Azarenka serve side extremely well.

One of the best rookie backs!

Baller, very good hands and throws. Still needs to pick up some decision making but seems to process fast (first day) too much not serving player first

Lining up for autograph on court in uniform
Not looking around court between points.

Moved during the point and kept touching the net as he was running around it. Kept turning his head at the net despite being told multiple times not to. He's excited and trying, but not going very well. He asked for feedback multiple times at least.
Low effort and not very focused. Taking his time to roll
Showed up late to his stadium shift and took a while to get his uniform back on. Lacks maturity
still slow in recognition/awareness, causes him to get out of position, hesitate. He’s definitely trying to keep track of things, he just needs to grow into the pace of play.

rolls can be wildly inconsistent at times. Just sent one right between the two backs, most aren’t that bad tho. Fairly aware, maybe a bet too jumpy at times? He should get better but we gotta fix those rolls
Decent first day. Working on decision making and rolling. Rolls very bouncy and off target. Strong communication though, always looking both ways and checking where all the balls are.
short stint but looks like he’s aware and making good decisions. Rolls need work. Also doing a weird thing where receives a role like a catcher trying to lock home plate. Good for a first day!
Good to have as a solid veteran
Really good!
Good court awarness, did miss one freeze frame, but she stepped up her game for the rest of the day after that. Quick balancing. Ready for the balls to change sides, even before the nets sometimes

So good today again
Yeah, she’s very good. Helpful. Does nothing wrong.
Sharp, fast, aware
doing a good job stepping out to serve player/receive balls back. She looks confident out there. Locked in tiebreak. Considered a 4, I’m not quite there yet but she really looks good.
Got better throughout day with balancing. Generally pretty good
Good hands, good hustle. Whole team needed to come in on changeovers faster
Put her on finals! Amazing!
Wonderful performance. Looks very strong out there. Very reliable.

Michelle is a good back. She's quick and has good hands and dexterity. Needs to perhaps show hands more, look at partner and net to make sure she has assessed the entire court. Overall, good back and well behaved.
good hands! Michelle is making a few more mistakes this year than I remember last year. The good is still really good but occasional lapses in concentration.
great athlete. Good throwing balances. Situational awareness is good. Would like to see her step further from back when serving player, and start thinking more about pre-balancing.
very solid. good reflexes and decision-making, not rusty at all on day 1

hesitating and making some wrong decisions on crossing/balls between net and back that are leading to confusion with net partner and back. Actually he’s getting out of position constantly.

Good attitude. Seems to enjoy it and communicate, and ask questions. Only really had one shift with him, still learning but ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
Very solid on stadium

Solid. Some slowness but knows where everything needs to happen

pretty good other than a few minor hiccups. Wasn’t balancing the balls on the warmup, just sending everything to my end. Very high effort, good speed and athleticism, good attitude. Good second year net

Looking really strong all around. Really nice speed and rolls. Seems to have a good understanding of what’s going on
Good hustle. No major mistakes
Seeing really good hustle. Running back to his position. Made a really smart move to run to the net to roll to the back on a service change because the net was busy getting a ball in the net

Good shifts all day. Nice rolls, shows hands, looks both ways every time. After changeover he ran on across right as Davis was coming around the chair and he ran into her. But just a stupid accident, and now he’ll never forget to look before running across.
Very good at receiving rolls.
Did a good job on a tiebreak on stadium. A few minor issues such as not rolling a ball before retrieving a new one, momentary hesitation when players switch sides

Overall good day. His communication and court awarness improved considerably over day. Good freeze frame. Two good tiebreaks but still working on rolling the ball he has to the back first before running to get the ball last in play.
Good net stadium shift for a rookie. Some hesitation on when to cross but quick reaction time
Solid net. Fit in well with a team of 5 captains for a late night shift. Did not look out of place.
Really great consistent performance. Brought the intensity.
Did a great job of applying a comment from Fergus. Rolled the ball in his hand first during a service change before getting the rest of the balls from the back
good net, fast, good hands. decent pacing (sometimes too fast) but very solid

Good awareness and really good rolls. Loud stomps when running

great for a first year. Learns quick and makes good decisions.

athletic, figured out to stop rolling the ball when the back wasn’t looking on court 4 since it was going into the wet grass. Running backwards back to his position, needs to turn and run back, not max effort. Solid rookie but some maturity concerns for me as well that make me nervous behaviorally.
Solid, but subpar tosses and occasional inaccurate balance
missed a few balancing opportunities with Benjy when he was looking to make a late balance but she didn’t see him. Another bad balance/roll later. Still looks quite good but making some errors.
No major mistakes. Rolls can be great, but can be inconsistent at times.
presenting between serves. Didn’t shift for service warmups. Rolled between serves.

Emily is enthusiastic but perhaps too confident in her knowledge and ability. She tends to interject and impose her will when it isn't needed or requested. She kept taking charge when she should have been following instructions and learning.
She is athletic, fast, and has ability, she just needs to interject herself a bit less. Using this comparison again....I would not have her on my work team.
Really solid but not necessarily lights out
Not bad, made some decisions that were a bit odd for me at times
Great awareness and aggression. Ran to the back after getting a ball while it was def closer to go back to net
Great job on new balls taking command and getting them to the correct side
Exhibiting fantastic awareness. Great work with Sachin
Great hands, knows what she is doing generally. Was just hesitant on some rolls she could have made while player was in area, held instead of rolling away.

Shaky start, still hesitant at times. Needs to work on on court presence/assertiveness. Did a great job not rolling in the player's feet and not rolling between 1st and 2nd serve. Could roll a bit faster. Has good potential.

Very athletic and has good feel for what balls to go after. But very bad roll led to point being delayed. Niceeee (very helpful to have her on the team). I don’t believe she (or anyone else on the team) added comments during our post-shift talks, but it was extremely helpful to have her on court, and felt like she was the next most veteran or dependable person on my time

Really good!

great net. fast, good paced rolls, good awareness. helped tiebreak run very smoothly

Matthew is a nice kid and is receptive to advice on how to improve. He just needs more time and confidence. This was discussed with all parties involved and it did improve.
